title: BWC & Incident Records: Deputy Harodis Nunez Shot in Chest During Family Disturbance – Davenport, FL (October 2022)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the shooting of Deputy Harodis Nunez during a family disturbance call.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: October 2022
Location: Tanglewood Preserve subdivision, Davenport, Polk County, Florida
Suspect Name: Gabriel Batista, 41
Victim Officer: Deputy Harodis Nunez (shot in the chest, saved by bulletproof vest)
 
Incident Summary: Deputies responded to a family disturbance at approximately 10 p.m. While deputies were on scene, suspect Gabriel Batista returned home and drove past two marked patrol cars, confronting two deputies in the front yard. Batista kept his hands behind his back and refused to show them when deputies asked. He then shot Deputy Nunez once in the chest. The deputy's bulletproof vest saved his life. Deputies returned fire. Nunez was released from the hospital two days later. Sheriff Grady Judd released bodycam footage at a press conference.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Deputy Nunez and all other responding deputies involved in the disturbance call and shooting.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles at the scene.
 
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to the initial disturbance call.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Fatal Shooting of Senior Airman Roger Fortson – Fort Walton Beach, FL (May 2024)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the fatal shooting of Senior Airman Roger Fortson by an Okaloosa County Sheriff's deputy.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: May 3, 2024
Location: Apartment 1401, apartment complex in Fort Walton Beach, Okaloosa County, Florida
Victim: Senior Airman Roger Fortson, 23 (deceased)
Arresting Agency: Okaloosa County Sheriff's Office
 
Incident Summary: A deputy responded to a reported disturbance at the apartment complex at approximately 4:28 p.m. A woman at the complex told the deputy she heard yelling and possibly a slap coming from apartment 1401. The deputy took the elevator to the fourth floor, knocked three times, and announced "Sheriff's office, open the door" twice. Within seconds of Fortson opening the door, the deputy said "Step back" and fired his weapon, fatally shooting Fortson. Fortson was holding a legally owned firearm at his side. He later died from his injuries. The victim's family, represented by attorney Ben Crump, disputed claims about the incident.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from the shooting deputy and all other deputies who responded to the apartment complex.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles at the scene.
 
The complete incident report, including all officer narratives and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings related to the initial disturbance report, including the call that prompted the deputy's response.
 
All dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.
 
Any statements taken from witnesses at the apartment complex.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Deputy Dragged From SUV & Fatal Shooting of Paul Smith – Hudson, FL (March 2024)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the fatal deputy-involved shooting of Paul Smith following an incident in which a deputy was dragged from Smith's SUV.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: March 16, 2024
Location: 7-Eleven gas station, corner of US 19 and New York Avenue, Hudson, Pasco County, Florida
Suspect Name: Paul Smith, 40 (deceased)
Arresting Agency: Pasco County Sheriff's Office
 
Incident Summary: A deputy on patrol recognized Smith, who was wanted for battery by strangulation and had an extensive criminal history including a 2019 arrest for battery on a law enforcement officer, escape, and resisting with violence. When the deputy approached, Smith became violent, got into his vehicle, and sped off with the deputy hanging from the SUV. The deputy repeatedly shouted commands while tasing Smith and warning "You're gonna get shot." The deputy then fired on Smith while being dragged. Smith was taken to the hospital where he later died. The deputy suffered minor injuries and was discharged the same night.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from the deputy involved in the shooting and all other responding deputies.
 
All dashcam and in-car camera footage from all patrol vehicles at the scene.
 
The complete incident report, including all officer narratives and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.
 
Any surveillance footage from the 7-Eleven obtained by investigators.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Deputy Greg McCowan Killed & Deputy Shelby Eggers Wounded During Traffic Stop – Blount County, TN (February 2024)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the fatal shooting of Deputy Greg McCowan and the wounding of Deputy Shelby Eggers during a traffic stop.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: February 2024
Location: Blount County, Tennessee
Victim Officers: Deputy Greg McCowan (killed); Deputy Shelby Eggers (seriously wounded)
Suspect Name: Kenneth Wayne DeHart
 
Incident Summary: Deputy Eggers initiated a traffic stop on a vehicle driven by DeHart after observing the vehicle crossing the center line multiple times. During the stop, Eggers suspected marijuana use and asked DeHart to exit. The situation escalated and DeHart opened fire, fatally shooting Deputy McCowan and seriously wounding Deputy Eggers. DeHart fled the scene, triggering a multi-day manhunt before being taken into custody. Sheriff Berrong released bodycam and dashcam footage from both deputies for transparency. McCowan had been with the department since 2020 and received a lifesaving commendation in 2021.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Deputy McCowan, Deputy Eggers, and any other officers who responded to the scene.
 
All dashcam footage from both deputies' patrol vehicles.
 
The complete incident reports, including all officer narratives.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to the traffic stop, the shooting, and the subsequent manhunt.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Marija Malesevic.

title: BWC & Incident Records: Sgt. Dustin DeMonte & Officer Alex Hamzy Killed in Ambush, Officer Iurato Wounded – Bristol, CT (October 2022)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the ambush shooting that killed two Bristol police officers and wounded a third.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: October 12, 2022
Location: Bristol, Connecticut
Victim Officers: Sgt. Dustin DeMonte, 35 (killed); Officer Alex Hamzy (killed); Officer Alec Iurato, 26 (wounded)
Suspect Name: Nicholas Brutcher, 35 (deceased)
 
Incident Summary: Officers responded to a 911 call shortly before 11 p.m. Authorities believe the call was made by the suspect, Nicholas Brutcher, to lure officers to his home. Upon arrival, Brutcher opened fire in an ambush, killing Sgt. DeMonte and Officer Hamzy. Officer Iurato was shot in the leg but, despite his injuries, hobbled behind a patrol cruiser and fired a single shot at the suspect, fatally striking him. The wounded officer then radioed "Shots fired, shots fired, more cars, send everyone." DeMonte was a 10-year veteran and co-recipient of the department's 2019 Officer of the Year award.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Officer Alec Iurato, Sgt. Dustin DeMonte, Officer Alex Hamzy, and all other officers who responded to the scene.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles at the scene.
 
The complete incident reports, including all officer narratives and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings, including the initial call allegedly made by the suspect to lure officers.
 
All dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Officer Tara O'Sullivan Fatally Shot in Ambush & 8-Hour Standoff – Sacramento, CA (June 2019)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the fatal ambush shooting of Officer Tara O'Sullivan and the subsequent eight-hour standoff with the suspect.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: June 19, 2019
Location: Redwood Avenue, North Sacramento, California
Victim Officer: Officer Tara O'Sullivan, 26 (killed in the line of duty)
Suspect Name: Adel Sambrano Ramos, 45 (arrested)
 
Incident Summary: Officer O'Sullivan and other officers were assisting a woman in moving her belongings from a home when suspect Ramos opened fire with a high-powered rifle, firing over 20 shots. O'Sullivan was struck and fatally wounded. Bodycam captured an officer attempting to talk the suspect down before the shooting erupted. The suspect barricaded himself for approximately eight hours before surrendering. The department was criticized for taking 45 minutes to rescue O'Sullivan due to waiting for an armored vehicle. Ramos was charged with murder, attempted murder, and possessing illegal weapons. He had multiple firearms including two assault rifles.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Officer O'Sullivan and all other Sacramento PD officers who responded to the initial call, were present during the shooting, and participated in the standoff.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles at the scene.
 
The complete incident reports, including all officer narratives and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

Incident Date: February 2022
Location: Sarasota, Florida
Suspect Name: Martin Hyde (Republican candidate for Florida's 16th Congressional District)

Incident Summary: Officer Beskin stopped Hyde for speeding and texting while driving. Hyde immediately told her "You know who I am, right?" and threatened to call her chief. He refused to cooperate with requests for registration and became increasingly aggressive and confrontational. The bodycam captured the entire encounter. Hyde is a political figure backed by Roger Stone and Michael Flynn who had made multiple runs for Sarasota City Commission.

All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles at the scene.

title: BWC & Incident Records: Sherita Dixon-Cole DUI Arrest & False Sexual Assault Accusation Against State Trooper – Ellis County, TX (May 2018)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the DUI arrest of Sherita Dixon-Cole and her subsequent false accusation of sexual assault against the arresting state trooper.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: May 2018
Location: Ellis County, Texas
Suspect Name: Sherita Dixon-Cole, 37
Arresting Agency: Texas Department of Public Safety (State Trooper)
 
Incident Summary: Dixon-Cole was pulled over and arrested for DUI after failing a field sobriety test. Following her arrest, she accused the state trooper of offering to let her go without charges if she performed sexual favors, and claimed he sexually assaulted her after she declined. The case made national headlines. The Ellis County Sheriff's Office released approximately two hours of unedited bodycam footage showing the entire interaction — from the traffic stop through the sobriety tests to booking at the Ellis County Jail. The footage showed a routine and professional DUI arrest with no misconduct. Dixon-Cole's attorney later publicly apologized for his client's false claims.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from the arresting state trooper covering the entire traffic stop, field sobriety testing, arrest, transport, and booking of Sherita Dixon-Cole.
 
All dashcam footage from the trooper's patrol vehicle during the traffic stop and transport.
 
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives.
 
All dispatch/CAD logs related to the traffic stop.
 
Any booking or intake video/audio recordings from the Ellis County Jail.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Undercover Officers from Two Precincts Confront Each Other During Drug Operation – Detroit, MI (November 2017)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Michigan Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as incident reports, regarding the incident in which undercover officers from two Detroit Police precincts unknowingly confronted each other during overlapping drug operations, resulting in a physical altercation.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: November 9, 2017
Location: 19000 block of Andover Street, Detroit, Michigan (east side)
Units Involved: Detroit PD 11th Precinct special operations unit; Detroit PD 12th Precinct undercover unit
 
Incident Summary: Officers from the 12th Precinct were conducting an undercover operation posing as drug dealers to arrest buyers. Separately, officers from the 11th Precinct arrived to execute a search warrant on the same suspected drug house, unaware of the 12th Precinct's operation. Two 11th Precinct officers first encountered two undercover 12th Precinct officers and attempted to detain them at gunpoint. When the 11th Precinct team entered the home to execute their warrant, 12th Precinct officers stormed in, triggering a brawl between the two groups. Bodycam captured officers shouting "Don't put your hand on your gun" and "We have a search warrant." One officer was taken to a hospital for injuries.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from all 11th Precinct and 12th Precinct officers involved in the incident, including the initial confrontation, the entry into the residence, and the physical altercation.
 
All dashcam footage from all patrol and unmarked vehicles at the scene.
 
The complete incident reports, including all officer narratives and internal affairs documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to the search warrant execution and any calls for backup.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Jhasmynn Sheppard Arrest & Use of Force – Tuscaloosa, AL (April 2019)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the arrest of Jhasmynn Alexiss Sheppard following a hit-and-run, which generated significant public concern after bystander and bodycam footage was released.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: April 19, 2019
Location: Tuscaloosa, Alabama
Suspect Name: Jhasmynn Alexiss Sheppard
Involved Officers: Officers Ward and Lackey (identified by last name only)
 
Incident Summary: Officers responded to a call regarding someone leaving the scene of an accident at approximately 6:40 p.m. About 25 minutes later, officers located the suspect vehicle driven by Sheppard and conducted a traffic stop. After pulling Sheppard from the vehicle, a physical confrontation ensued. Officers used a baton and made threatening statements captured on bodycam audio. Sheppard was charged with resisting arrest, second-degree assault, and disarming law enforcement. Bystander video went viral on social media, and the Tuscaloosa Police Department released bodycam footage. The police chief stated the video was "offensive" and did not reflect department training.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Officers Ward, Lackey, and any other officers who responded to or were present during the arrest of Jhasmynn Sheppard.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles present during the incident.
 
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to the initial hit-and-run report and the traffic stop.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the fatal shooting of Deputy Isaiah Cordero during a traffic stop and the subsequent multi-county pursuit that ended in a shootout.

Suspect Name: William Shae McKay (deceased)

Incident Summary: Deputy Cordero attempted a traffic stop on a vehicle driven by McKay. As Cordero approached the vehicle, McKay opened fire, killing the deputy. McKay fled, prompting a massive multi-county pursuit through San Bernardino and Riverside Counties involving dozens of police vehicles. Officers used spike strips and PIT maneuvers. The pursuit ended when McKay's truck came to a stop under an interstate overpass in Norco. A shootout ensued and McKay was fatally shot.

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the officer-involved shooting in which a Fresno PD sergeant was ambushed and shot three times while investigating a homicide, and the suspect was fatally shot.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: October 26, 2022
Location: South Seventh Street near East Verrue Avenue; East Platt Avenue and Eighth Street, Fresno, California
Suspect Name: Andy Morales, 40 (deceased)
Victim Officer: Sergeant (name withheld in public reports, referred to as "Sergeant #1")
 
Incident Summary: A sergeant was parked on South Seventh Street investigating gunshots detected by ShotSpotter and a nearby homicide. A blue Nissan Frontier driven by Morales rolled up and the suspect opened fire, striking the patrol car at least 10 times, hitting the sergeant three times in the legs. Despite his injuries, the sergeant made a U-turn and pursued Morales, who fired again near an elementary school. The shootout lasted over one minute before two additional officers arrived. Morales was fatally shot. He was also suspected of the nearby homicide.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from the ambushed sergeant and all other responding Fresno PD officers involved in the pursuit and shooting.
 
All dashcam footage from all patrol vehicles involved.
 
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings, ShotSpotter alert data, and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

title: BWC & Incident Records: Daniel Allen Vehicle Ramming, Officer-Involved Shooting & 9-Hour SWAT Standoff – Louisville, KY (March 2019)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the vehicle ramming, officer-involved shooting, and nine-hour SWAT standoff involving suspect Daniel Allen.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: March 28, 2019
Location: 1400 block of Weyler Avenue and 3000 block of Montana Avenue, Taylor Berry neighborhood, Louisville, Kentucky
Suspect Name: Daniel C. Allen, 26
Shooting Officer: Officer Robert Tyler Holland
 
Incident Summary: Officers located Allen, wanted on six felony warrants, hiding in a stolen van on Weyler Avenue. When officers surrounded the van, Allen climbed to the front, started the vehicle, and attempted to flee — ramming police vehicles and nearly striking multiple officers. Officer Holland fired multiple rounds, striking Allen. Allen fled in the van approximately one mile to Montana Avenue, abandoned it, and barricaded himself inside a home. SWAT, the Hostage Negotiation Team, and the bomb squad were deployed. Three occupants were removed from the home without incident. After approximately nine hours, SWAT officers used a chainsaw to cut a hole in a second-story wall and found Allen hiding in the ceiling rafters with a gunshot wound. He was charged with four counts of attempted murder of a police officer and nearly two dozen other charges.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Officer Holland and all other LMPD officers and detectives involved in the initial contact, van ramming, shooting, and pursuit to Montana Avenue.
 
All BWC footage from SWAT officers who responded to and entered the barricaded home.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles present at both locations.
 
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives and use-of-force reports.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

communications.communication:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the multi-county pursuit, standoff, and stolen cruiser incident involving suspect Jeremy Reynolds.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: March 6, 2022
Location: Richland County, Ohio — Millsboro Road / Lexington-Springmill Road area; U.S. 30; State Route 511
Suspect Name: Jeremy Reynolds, 31
Agencies Involved: Ontario Police Department, Ohio State Highway Patrol
 
Incident Summary: Troopers attempted to stop a suspected stolen vehicle driven by Reynolds. He struck several vehicles during the pursuit, causing his car to break down on Millsboro Road. A 40-minute standoff ensued. Reynolds then escaped using smoke from the disabled vehicle as cover, stole an unlocked Ohio State Highway Patrol cruiser — with a woman still in the back seat — and drove off despite being shot twice. He later abandoned the cruiser, carjacked a minivan, and led officers on a second chase until troopers intentionally rammed the minivan to end the pursuit.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from Ontario PD officers and Ohio State Highway Patrol troopers involved in the initial pursuit, standoff, shooting, and second pursuit.
 
All dashcam and in-car camera footage from all patrol vehicles involved.
 
The complete incident and arrest reports, including all officer narratives.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

Specific details to assist your search:
 
Incident Date: November 17, 2024
Location: Sarasota, Florida
Suspect Name: Sophia Ross, 22
Arresting Agency: Sarasota Police Department
 
Incident Summary: Officers pulled Ross over after observing her stopping at green lights and striking several curbs. Upon contact, Ross was swaying off balance, slurring her words, and acting lethargic. She admitted to having been drinking at a bar. During the traffic stop, Ross repeatedly called the officer "babe" and "daddy," attempted to kiss the officer, flashed her Hooters uniform top, and tried to flirt her way out of the arrest. She initially refused field sobriety tests saying "I'm literally drinking and not gonna drive" and denied she had been driving. She was arrested and charged with DUI. Her blood alcohol content was 0.318, nearly four times the legal limit.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from all officers who responded to and interacted with Sophia Ross during the traffic stop, field sobriety testing, arrest, and transport.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles present during the stop.
 
The complete incident and arrest report, including all officer narratives.

Any booking or intake video/audio recordings from the jail.

Sincerely,

title: BWC & Incident Records: Bay County Deputy Shootout With Armed Suspect – Bay County, FL

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am submitting this request on behalf of my professional YouTube news and educational channel that reports on police interactions, body camera footage, public safety incidents, and transparency in law enforcement. Our content is journalistic in nature, aimed at informing the public about police procedures and accountability through factual, narrated analysis of public records.
 
I am requesting all available body-worn camera (BWC) and dashcam footage, as well as the initial incident report, regarding the fatal officer-involved shooting in which a Bay County deputy engaged an armed suspect who drew a handgun on the deputy during a roadside encounter.
 
Specific details to assist your search:
 
Location: Bay County, Florida
Suspect: Armed male carrying a shotgun over his shoulder and a handgun holstered on his left side (deceased)
Arresting Agency: Bay County Sheriff's Office
 
Incident Summary: A Bay County deputy made contact with an armed male subject during a roadside encounter. The suspect became increasingly agitated, questioned how the deputy knew his name, and asked if the body camera was on. The deputy asked to remove the shotgun slung across the suspect's shoulder. The suspect refused and made threatening statements. The situation rapidly escalated when the suspect grabbed a handgun holstered on his left side and pointed it at the deputy. Both the deputy and suspect exchanged gunfire on both sides of the patrol vehicle. The suspect fell to the ground and continued firing from the grass. The deputy fired additional rounds over the hood of the car and then moved in to disarm the suspect and render aid. The suspect was pronounced dead at the scene.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from the deputy involved and all other deputies who responded to the scene.
 
All dashcam footage from patrol vehicles at the scene.
 
The complete incident report, including all officer narratives and use-of-force documentation.
 
All 911 call recordings and dispatch/CAD logs related to this incident.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Marija Malesevic

Location: Emmet County, Michigan
Suspect Name: U.S. District Judge Thomas Ludington, 72

Incident Summary: Judge Ludington, a George W. Bush appointee to the federal bench, crashed his Cadillac and was arrested for operating while intoxicated. His blood alcohol content was 0.27 — more than three times the legal limit of 0.08. Bodycam footage shows Ludington slurring his words, denying having anything to drink, claiming he didn't know how his car crashed or why his airbags deployed, and having difficulty following police instructions. Incident Date: May 29, 2017
Location: Military Trail, Jupiter, Palm Beach County, Florida
Suspect Name: Eldrick Tont "Tiger" Woods
Arresting Agency: Jupiter Police Department
 
Incident Summary: In the early morning hours, officers found Woods asleep behind the wheel of his running Mercedes-Benz, which was parked awkwardly on the roadway with two flat tires and damage to the front and rear bumpers. Officers had to wake him. Woods appeared disoriented and failed field sobriety tests. He was arrested for DUI. A breathalyzer showed 0.000 BAC, but toxicology later revealed five prescription drugs in his system including Vicodin, Dilaudid, Xanax, Ambien, and THC. He later pled guilty to reckless driving.
 
Records requested:
 
All BWC footage from responding and arresting officers who interacted with Tiger Woods during the traffic encounter, field sobriety testing, arrest, and transport.

title: Shannon McMinimee (Tacoma Public Schools)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Shannon McMinimee (Tacoma Public Schools)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All public records relating to the hiring, employment, contract terms, performance, complaints, concerns, allegations, investigations, affiliations, and separation/termination of Shannon McMinimee, including but not limited to:

1. Every document (including meeting minutes, emails, text messages, memos, notes, or other written communication) regarding the recruitment, selection, and hiring process for the position filled by Shannon McMinimee, including records regarding any other applicants or candidates who were considered or interviewed for the same position.

2. Every document (including meeting minutes, emails, text messages, memos, notes, or other written communication) regarding her interview process, offer of employment, contract negotiations, onboarding, and appointment.

3. All documents relating to any background check, investigation, or vetting process conducted before or after her hiring, including but not limited to:
a. Communications or records showing what information was sought or obtained about her prior employment history, reasons for separation from any prior employers, or any disciplinary or termination actions at previous workplaces;
b. Any information regarding litigation, claims, settlements, or lawsuits filed by or against Shannon McMinimee and any payouts received;
c. Any documents, emails, or communications about her affiliations or membership in any organizations, including the John Brown Gun Club or other groups, and whether those affiliations were reviewed or disclosed;
d. Identification of who conducted or oversaw any such investigation or vetting process, and any correspondence about their findings or recommendations.

4. Copies of her employment contract(s), amendments, addenda, resignation letter(s), termination notices, settlement or separation agreements, and any related Board resolutions or votes.

5. Every document (including meeting minutes, emails, text messages, memos, notes, or other written communication) regarding her job duties, performance evaluations, contract extensions or amendments.

6. Every document (including meeting minutes, emails, text messages, memos, notes, or other written communication) regarding any complaints, concerns, grievances, allegations, or reports — whether formal or informal, whether or not investigated — involving Shannon McMinimee, and any related actions, follow-up, or resolution.

7. Every document (including meeting minutes, emails, text messages, memos, notes, or other written communication) regarding her resignation, non-renewal, termination, separation, or any other departure-related discussions.

8. Any communications between Board Members, the Superintendent, HR staff, or legal counsel discussing her recruitment, hiring, background, prior employment, affiliations, litigation history, job performance, contract terms, complaints, concerns, allegations, investigations, or termination.

9. Any records showing payments, settlements, severance, or other financial arrangements related to her employment or separation.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please provide a privilege/exemption log as required by RCW 42.56.210(3), identifying the specific exemption and the basis for it.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Public Interest Mainstreaming Parenting
